body.scope:

- RAG pipeline design and implementation using FastAPI

- Hybrid retrieval combining semantic and keyword search
- Custom NLP processing for legal domain text

- Documentation and knowledge transfer

Not Included:
- Ongoing hosting or maintenance post-delivery
- Manual data labeling or annotation
- Development of front-end interfaces

milestoneFrameworkJson.deliverable_statement: Ingested and indexed knowledge base: 200k Zendesk logs and 50k product catalogue entries are processed, chunked, embedded, and stored in a vector database, with automated quality checks confirming over 95% successful ingestion.

Functional RAG prototype: A working API endpoint that, given a sample of 100 real customer questions, retrieves relevant source documents and generates answers with citations, achieving at least 85% accuracy against a human-validated test set.

Integrated, production-ready system: The RAG chatbot is connected to the live Zendesk and PostgreSQL sources via REST API, deployed on AWS ECS, and handles a simulated load of 2,000 daily queries with 95% of responses delivered in under 3 seconds.

Monitoring and escalation dashboard: A live dashboard displays real-time answer accuracy, source citation rate, and latency. An automated escalation pipeline routes low-confidence answers (and user-flagged failures) to human agents and logs all failures for weekly review.

sdlc_notices: Data Quality Risk: The existing Zendesk conversation logs (200k) may contain outdated, conflicting, or low-quality answers that could poison the knowledge base. A data cleaning and curation phase is mandatory before indexing.

Integration Dependency: The system relies on live REST API connections to Zendesk and the product catalogue. Any downtime or schema changes in these source systems will directly impact chatbot performance.
Latency Budget: Achieving the 3-second end-to-end target (including retrieval, generation, and network time) is tight and will require careful optimization of embedding search and model inference.

Success Metric Gap: The current target is 'accuracy,' but this needs a precise, measurable definition (e.g., human-rated correctness on a fixed test set, factuality score) agreed upon before Milestone 2 sign-off.
artifactAJson.business_intent: Build a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) chatbot for internal support agents that pulls answers directly from the Zendesk knowledge base and product catalogue, replacing the current error-prone system. The goal is to increase answer accuracy from 71% to over 90%, provide source citations to rebuild trust, and implement measurable quality tracking.

stack_tags: Embedding Model (e.g., OpenAI, Cohere, or open-source)
artifactBJson.legacy_volume: 200k Zendesk conversation logs and 50k SKU product catalogue entries. Initial indexing volume is ~250k documents. Query load is ~2,000 requests per day (approx. 1 query per 4 seconds during business hours), which is a low-to-moderate read load for a vector database.
artifactBJson.integration_method: The RAG system will be deployed as a containerized service on AWS ECS. It will expose a FastAPI endpoint for the support agent interface. On receiving a query, it will: 1) call a vector similarity search against the indexed knowledge base, 2) call the Zendesk and PostgreSQL REST APIs in parallel for any fresh or dynamic data, 3) construct a prompt with retrieved context for the LLM, and 4) return the cited answer. Redis will be used for caching frequent queries and session context.
